question: Using only this graph, which companies use recurring subscriptions for offerings under Automation Suite?
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name
MATCH (o)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company

WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies AND s.name IN $segments
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

question: Using only the supplied dataset, list the business segments at Vector Industrial whose company operates in the German market and that serve software builders and sell through digital channels.
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name AND company.name IN $companies
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

WITH DISTINCT company

Using only this graph, name the business segments at Vector Industrial named Supply Chain that serve industrial companies and sell through reseller partners.
gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies AND s.name IN $segments
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
MATCH (s)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

Based only on the available knowledge graph, what are the top 5 channels by segment count within segments at Vector Industrial?
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)
WHERE company.name IN $companies
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name

RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company LIMIT 2

Using only this graph, which channels are in the top 3 by segment usage within segments at Vector Industrial operating in Germany?
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
WHERE company.name IN $companies
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

Based only on the available knowledge graph, top 5 channels by segment count within segments at Vector Industrial operating in the Japanese market.
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
WHERE company.name IN $companies
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(channel:Channel)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
RETURN channel.name AS channel, COUNT(DISTINCT [company.name, s.name]) AS segment_count
ORDER BY segment_count DESC, channel
LIMIT $limit

MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s2:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s2.company_name = c.name
MATCH (s2)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_2})
RETURN DISTINCT c.name AS company ORDER BY company

From this KG snapshot alone, list the business segments for companies operating in the Australian market that serve public-sector bodies.
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s.company_name = company.name
MATCH (s)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company, s.name AS segment ORDER BY company, segment

question: Using only this graph, does Vector Industrial serve IT teams and end users and sell through device makers across its segments?
gold_cypher:
MATCH (c:Company)
WHERE c.name IN $companies
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s1:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s1.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
MATCH (s1)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_1})
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s2:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s2.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
MATCH (s2)-[:SERVES]->(:CustomerType {name: $customer_type_2})
MATCH (c:Company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(s3:BusinessSegment)
WHERE s3.company_name = c.name AND c.name IN $companies
MATCH (s3)-[:SELLS_THROUGH]->(:Channel {name: $channel_3})
RETURN COUNT(DISTINCT c.name) > 0 AS is_match

question: From this KG snapshot alone, list the companies operating in the German market whose offerings under Automation Suite use recurring subscriptions.
gold_cypher:
MATCH (company:Company)-[:OPERATES_IN]->(place:Place)
WHERE any(requested_place IN $places WHERE requested_place = place.name OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.includes_places, []) OR requested_place IN coalesce(place.within_places, []))
WITH DISTINCT company
MATCH (company)-[:HAS_SEGMENT]->(:BusinessSegment)-[:OFFERS]->(root:Offering)
MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name
MATCH (o)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company

MATCH (root)-[:OFFERS*0..]->(o:Offering)
WHERE root.name IN $offerings AND root.company_name = company.name AND o.company_name = company.name
MATCH (o)-[:MONETIZES_VIA]->(:RevenueModel {name: $revenue_model})
RETURN DISTINCT company.name AS company ORDER BY company
